name: Fireline Cedar

pnw_source.common_name: Western red cedar under prescribed burn
pnw_source.ecological_role: Fire ecology in cedar forests: prescribed burns clear understory, recycle nutrients, trigger seed germination, and prevent catastrophic wildfire. Cedar bark is fire-resistant at maturity but vulnerable when young — fire management is a balance between destruction and renewal

description: Burn perimeter, pyrotechnics safety, fire management. Fireline Cedar manages the most dangerous operation in the city: the burn itself. Like prescribed fire in Pacific Northwest forests — where controlled destruction prevents catastrophic loss and triggers renewal — this camp manages the boundary between creative destruction and safety, ensuring that when skills are promoted (burned), the perimeter holds.

member_skills.role: Burn perimeter establishment and safety line management
Burn timing coordination and ignition chain management
Post-burn assessment and nutrient cycling analysis

notes: Fireline Cedar is the only camp with 5 member skills (the maximum for any non-medical camp). Fire management demands depth. The camp requires Level 3 trust (Old Growth) because burn operations are irreversible — skill promotion and deprecation cannot be undone. The fire-succession art installation archetype can only emerge when Fireline Cedar and Cedar Grove skills compose, reflecting the ecological truth that fire and growth are the same cycle.
